mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% in Sept 2011 indicates elevated unemployment from post-2008 recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) above its 6-month moving average indicates sustained consumer demand strength, which signals robust corporate revenue growth and positive equity risk premium, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 395,472M in Sept 2011 reflects post-crisis demand recovery
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary policy constraint and real rate compression, which reduces equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real returns deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in Sept 2011 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 in Aug 2011 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery plateau
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) combined with payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) signals broad-based demand expansion across residential and labor sectors, which drives positive equity returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 3-month moving average AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 50k
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 571k in Aug 2011 reflects housing recovery
fact:PAYEMS at 131.3M in Sept 2011 shows labor market stabilization
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and employment
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of HOUST and PAYEMS is not significantly stronger than either variable alone (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) below zero signals monetary accommodation that reduces required equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in low-rate regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero with positive inflation creates negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary easing with negative real rates
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the negative real rate signal produces no statistically significant coefficient in logistic regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) accelerating above 100k per month signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which increases corporate labor costs and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as profit margin expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131.3M in Sept 2011 reflects post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declining quarter-over-quarter signals economic deceleration and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,271.8B in Q2 2011 reflects post-crisis recovery deceleration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
